fact: March 19 sits exactly 78 days into the year, which means there are still 287 days left to go!
This date falls smack dab in the middle of spring fever season in the Northern Hemisphere, when animals get extra bouncy and start doing their weirdest mating dances.
On March 19, the sun rises almost exactly due east and sets almost exactly due west because we're so close to the spring equinox.
Tree sap is flowing like crazy around this time in cold countries, making it prime maple syrup season - some trees can produce 10 gallons of sap in a single day!
March 19 marks the peak time when hibernating bears start waking up super cranky and hungry after months of not eating anything.
The zodiac sign switches from Pisces to Aries right around this date, which astrologers say means the cosmic energy shifts from dreamy fish vibes to fiery ram energy.
Daylight and darkness are almost perfectly balanced on March 19, with each lasting about 12 hours - it's like nature's way of being fair to both day and night lovers.
This is prime time for the grossest seasonal phenomenon ever: billions of earthworms start wiggling up from underground as the soil thaws, creating squishy worm highways everywhere.
March 19 falls during the time when migrating whales are cruising past coastlines, and some species travel over 100 miles in a single day during their epic journeys.
The moon phase around March 19 changes every year, but when there's a full moon on this date, it creates some of the most dramatic spring tides of the year.
In the Southern Hemisphere, March 19 is deep autumn territory, when leaves are turning brilliant colors and falling like natural confetti.
This date sits in the sweet spot when ice on lakes and ponds starts making the weirdest creaking and groaning sounds as it begins to crack and shift with warming temperatures.
